source.domain: warehouse logistics, carts, conveyor accessories, pallet handling, tote systems, and material-flow hardware

requirements.part_name: Adjustable Conveyor Side Guide Bracket - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Adjustable Conveyor Side Guide Bracket'. The
broader use case is: Used along roller or belt conveyors in warehouses to keep cartons, totes, and
trays aligned during transport. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined
L-shaped bracket with elongated slots, clamp face, gusset ribs, and rail-mount holes. All other
product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this
mechanical role: Provides a rigid mounting point for a side guide rail with slotted adjustment for
product width changes. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests slots,
orthogonal mounting faces, rib-like reinforcements, clearance offsets, and repeated hole patterns in
a functional industrial bracket. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent
using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile,
machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called
out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 220 mm x 65 mm x 6 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 6 mm on the length centerline at X=13 mm and X=207 mm, Y=32 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 73 mm long x 8 mm wide through the part, centered at X=110 mm, Y=32 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 55 mm x 21 mm x 2 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.
acceptance_checklist: The output contains one continuous metal solid representing one part.
The stock family, material intent, envelope dimensions, and datum orientation match this requirement.
The feature named 'single rectangular metal body' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'mounting hole pattern' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'functional center feature' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'top relief pocket' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'edge chamfers' is present with the stated size and position.
All required chamfers, reliefs, and edge treatments are visible in STEP and STL outputs.
No extra parts, fasteners, hardware, labels, cosmetic meshes, or nonmetal components are present.
Flat datums remain planar and usable; pockets or slots do not accidentally break unsupported walls.
negative_requirements: Represent exactly one metal part only.
Do not model screws, nuts, springs, bushings, bearings, rubber pads, plastic covers, electronics, labels, handles, knobs, adhesives, weld beads, or separate inserts.
Do not convert the part into an assembly or multiple bodies.
Do not replace through holes, slots, pockets, grooves, flats, tapers, or chamfers with cosmetic markings.
Do not omit datum-critical features just because they are small.
Do not broaden the requirement back into the full product idea named 'Adjustable Conveyor Side Guide Bracket'; this requirement is only for its chosen metal part.

requirements.part_name: Pallet Stop Gate Pivot Arm - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Pallet Stop Gate Pivot Arm'. The broader use case
is: Installed at conveyor transfer points to stop or release pallets in controlled sequence. The
chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined pivot arm with a boss, through-bore,
stop pad face, actuator linkage hole, and relieved edges. All other product elements are external
reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Rotates into
the pallet path to block movement, then retracts when actuated by a cylinder or motor. It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests rotational geometry, concentric
bores, offset linkage features, contact faces, and asymmetric load-bearing shape. The part is made
from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using rectangular block stock. Start from one
rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets,
holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

requirements.part_name: Tote Nesting Locator Plate - single metal part

material.reason: low mass with good machinability and stable flat features

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Tote Nesting Locator Plate'. The broader use case
is: Mounted on workstations or carts to position plastic totes repeatably for scanning, picking, or
robotic handling. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Flat machined plate with
shallow pockets, raised locating pads, corner cutouts, countersunk mounting holes, and chamfers. All
other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform
this mechanical role: Locates the tote base using raised edges, corner reliefs, and chamfered lead-
in surfaces. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests shallow
pocketing, multiple height levels, chamfers, countersinks, and geometry that must match a container
footprint. The part is made from 6061-T6 aluminum using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat
sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief
features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not
separate welded pieces.

requirements.part_name: Heavy-Duty Cart Caster Mounting Yoke - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Heavy-Duty Cart Caster Mounting Yoke'. The broader
use case is: Used on warehouse carts, picking trolleys, and material handling dollies for reinforced
caster attachment. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined fork-style
yoke with two parallel lugs, axle holes, top mounting flange, and internal wheel clearance. All
other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform
this mechanical role: Holds a caster wheel or swivel assembly while transferring cart load into the
frame. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests forked geometry,
mirrored lug features, coaxial holes, internal clearances, fillets, and load-path proportions. The
part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using sheet or plate stock. Start from
one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and
relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet
part, not separate welded pieces.

requirements.part_name: Conveyor Roller End Bearing Housing - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Conveyor Roller End Bearing Housing'. The broader
use case is: Supports the end of a conveyor roller in a frame rail for carton or tote transport
systems. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined bearing block with
circular bearing pocket, shaft clearance bore, side mounting holes, and locating shoulder. All other
product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this
mechanical role: Holds a bearing or bushing concentric to the roller shaft while fastening into the
conveyor side frame. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests nested
cylindrical features, bearing seats, concentric holes, rectangular-to-round transitions, and
precision mounting geometry. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using
round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends,
bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts
or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

functional_features.name: single cylindrical body
functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 57 mm and length 63 mm from solid round bar.

functional_features.name: axial through bore
functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 19 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.

functional_features.name: end counterbores
functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 29 mm x 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: milled reference flat
functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 19 mm wide over 55 mm of length, centered on the top side.

functional_features.name: radial clamp holes
functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M5 holes through the top flat at X=21 mm and X=42 mm.

functional_features.name: split relief slit
functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 2 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.

functional_features.name: outside edge treatment
functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.

tolerances: Outside diameter and axial bore diameter: +/-0.05 mm.
Concentricity of bore to outer diameter: within 0.05 mm TIR.
Milled flats and slots: +/-0.15 mm unless otherwise specified.
Nonfunctional chamfers: +/-0.3 mm.
acceptance_checklist: The output contains one continuous metal solid representing one part.

requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Pallet Fork Entry Guide Shoe'. The broader use case
is: Attached to pallet handling fixtures or automated pallet dispensers to guide forklift tines into
position. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined tapered guide shoe with
flared entry ramps, bolt holes, wear face, and underside relief. All other product elements are
external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role:
Funnels fork tips into the correct channel while protecting pallet edges and fixture structure. It
is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests tapered solids, angled lead-in
faces, wear surfaces, countersunk fasteners, and robust industrial proportions. The part is made
from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using rectangular block stock. Start from one
rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets,
holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

source.raw_idea_title: Modular Flow Rack Lane Divider Clamp

requirements.part_name: Modular Flow Rack Lane Divider Clamp - single metal part

material.reason: durable wear surface for workshop loading

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Modular Flow Rack Lane Divider Clamp'. The broader
use case is: Used on carton flow racks to create adjustable lanes for bins, totes, or small cartons.
The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined split clamp block with
semicircular tube bore, pinch bolt holes, divider slot, and mounting boss. All other product
elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical
role: Clamps onto a cross tube or extrusion and supports a vertical lane divider rail. It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests split clamp geometry, partial
cylindrical cuts, slot-and-boss relationships, fastener clearances, and adjustable hardware logic.
The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized using round bar stock. Start from one cut
length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary
flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the
deliverable.

functional_features.name: single cylindrical body
functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 38 mm and length 118 mm from solid round bar.

functional_features.name: axial through bore
functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 8 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.

functional_features.name: end counterbores
functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 18 mm x 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: milled reference flat
functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 12 mm wide over 110 mm of length, centered on the top side.

functional_features.name: radial clamp holes
functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M8 holes through the top flat at X=39 mm and X=78 mm.

functional_features.name: split relief slit
functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 5 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.

functional_features.name: outside edge treatment
functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.

functional_features.name: cross relief slot
functional_features.description: Machine one transverse slot 7 mm wide across the top flat at X=59 mm, depth 3 mm.

tolerances: Outside diameter and axial bore diameter: +/-0.05 mm.
Concentricity of bore to outer diameter: within 0.05 mm TIR.
Milled flats and slots: +/-0.15 mm unless otherwise specified.
Nonfunctional chamfers: +/-0.3 mm.

source.domain: warehouse logistics, carts, conveyor accessories, pallet handling, tote systems, and material-flow hardware

requirements.part_name: Tote Transfer Corner Deflector - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Tote Transfer Corner Deflector'. The broader use
case is: Mounted at conveyor intersections where totes change direction or pass near frame corners.
The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined curved deflector block with
radiused outer face, mounting flange, countersunk holes, and relieved backside. All other product
elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical
role: Deflects tote edges smoothly around a corner to prevent snagging and impact damage. It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests curved contact surfaces, blended
radii, flange integration, asymmetric mounting, and collision-avoidance geometry. The part is made
from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet
or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features
into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate
welded pieces.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 90 mm x 80 mm x 3 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 5 mm on the length centerline at X=16 mm and X=74 mm, Y=40 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 30 mm long x 9 mm wide through the part, centered at X=45 mm, Y=40 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 25 mm x 26 mm x 2 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.

source.raw_idea_title: Pallet Lift Table Scissor Link Plate

requirements.part_name: Pallet Lift Table Scissor Link Plate - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Pallet Lift Table Scissor Link Plate'. The broader
use case is: Part of a warehouse lift table or pallet positioner used to raise loads for ergonomic
picking. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined flat link plate with
large pivot bores, spacer bosses, lightening pockets, and rounded ends. All other product elements
are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role:
Connects scissor lift pivots while maintaining spacing and carrying compressive and bending loads.
It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests long structural plates,
repeated pivot features, large-radius ends, pocketing, and symmetry around a load-bearing
centerline. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using round bar stock.
Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center,
then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are
part of the deliverable.

functional_features.name: single cylindrical body
functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 73 mm and length 26 mm from solid round bar.

functional_features.name: axial through bore
functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 14 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.

functional_features.name: end counterbores
functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 24 mm x 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: milled reference flat
functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 24 mm wide over 18 mm of length, centered on the top side.

functional_features.name: radial clamp holes
functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M8 holes through the top flat at X=8 mm and X=17 mm.

functional_features.name: split relief slit
functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 6 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.

functional_features.name: outside edge treatment
functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.

functional_features.name: opposed wrench flats
functional_features.description: Mill two opposed flats across the outside, leaving 65 mm across flats over the middle third of the length.

tolerances: Outside diameter and axial bore diameter: +/-0.05 mm.
Concentricity of bore to outer diameter: within 0.05 mm TIR.
Milled flats and slots: +/-0.15 mm unless otherwise specified.
Nonfunctional chamfers: +/-0.3 mm.

requirements.part_name: Cart Handle Height Adjustment Index Plate - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Cart Handle Height Adjustment Index Plate'. The
broader use case is: Used on warehouse picking carts or mobile workstations with adjustable push
handles. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined sector plate with arc-
pattern locking holes, central pivot bore, stop tab, and frame mounting holes. All other product
elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical
role: Provides indexed locking positions for a handle bracket at multiple ergonomic heights. It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests radial hole arrays, angular
constraints, pivot-centered geometry, tabs, and real adjustment mechanics. The part is made from
low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or
plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into
that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded
pieces.
